title: Has AI changed your trading discipline or execution workflow?
author: SupermarketAway5128
selftext:
I’ve been experimenting with AI tools to help generate setups and analyze patterns, but the real challenge I found was staying disciplined and not overtrading. What really helped me improve that part was trading in a structured environment like BullWaves Prime — not because of AI specifically, but because the rules made me slow down and respect my plan.

Now I find that combining AI signals with a solid execution routine keeps me focused instead of jumping at every suggestion. Curious how others use AI to stay disciplined and consistent in execution. Does AI help you avoid bad entries or does it sometimes make overtrading worse?

comments.author: BuildwithPublic
comments.body:
Completely changed the way I trade- profoundly. I can generate so many more ideas and strategies now, but actually get them live executing on an API in minutes now. Been mostly using Openclaw, perplexity agent skills on the r/publicapp api 

I’ve been posting some of my stuff in here will be building some more stuff over the next few days. You just want to keep building.
